description: GPU capacity intelligence: fleet state, workload character, MIG fragmentation, namespace compute share, allocation efficiency, long-term trends, and node balance. Use 7d-30d time range.

panels.description: Total GPU and MIG slice count

targets.expr: count(count by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED))

panels.title: Total Devices

panels.description: Devices with compute > 5%
panels.title: Busy GPUs

panels.description: VRAM in use but compute < 5% — model loaded, not working. Key waste signal.

targets.expr:
count(
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) > 0)
  unless
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100) > 5)
) or vector(0)

panels.title: Idle-Allocated GPUs

panels.description: No VRAM, no compute — fully free devices
panels.title: Unused GPUs

panels.description: Average GR engine active across all devices

targets.expr: avg(DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)

panels.title: Cluster Compute %

panels.description: Average VRAM utilization across all devices
panels.title: Cluster VRAM %

panels.description: Pods waiting for GPU resources — scheduling pressure signal
panels.title: Pending GPU Pods

panels.description: Demand signal: 0.6 × compute% + 0.4 × VRAM%. Indicates cluster saturation trend, not a true utilization measure.

targets.expr:
avg(
  (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100 * 0.6)
  + (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED / (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED + DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE) * 100 * 0.4)
)

panels.title: GPU Pressure Index

panels.description: Busy GPUs / Allocated GPUs × 100. Low value = many GPUs holding VRAM but not computing.

targets.expr:
(
  count(avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100) > 5)
  /
  count(avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) > 0)
) * 100 or vector(0)

panels.title: Allocation Efficiency

panels.description: Current snapshot: Busy (compute>5%) / Idle-Allocated (VRAM>0, compute<5%) / Unused

targets.expr:
count(
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) > 0)
  unless
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100) > 5)
) or vector(0)

panels.title: GPU State Distribution

panels.description: Stacked count of Busy / Idle-Allocated / Unused GPUs over time. Idle-Allocated growth signals efficiency degradation.

targets.expr:
count(
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) > 0)
  unless
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100) > 5)
) or vector(0)

panels.title: GPU State Over Time

panels.description: Memory bus utilization per device. Compare with Compute %: high DRAM + low compute = memory-bandwidth bound workload (common in LLM inference).
panels.title: DRAM Active % by Device

panels.description: FP16 pipe (mixed precision) and Tensor core utilization. Low FP16/Tensor on GPU designed for it = suboptimal workload configuration.
panels.title: FP16 & Tensor Pipe Active % by Device

panels.description: Sort by Compute % ASC to find memory-bound workloads. Pattern: high VRAM% + low Compute% + low DRAM% = idle inference server with model loaded.
panels.title: Workload Character Table

panels.description: % of MIG slices with no workload. High idle % = fragmentation or over-provisioning.

targets.expr:
(
  count(DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!=""})
  - (count(DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!="", exported_pod!=""}) or vector(0))
) / count(DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!=""}) * 100 or vector(0)

panels.title: MIG Idle %

panels.description: Total MIG slice count per profile. Reveals the partitioning strategy of the cluster.

targets.expr: count by (GPU_I_PROFILE) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!=""})

panels.title: MIG Slices by Profile

panels.description: Unallocated slices per profile. A profile with zero free slices cannot accept new workloads of that size — even if other profiles are idle.

targets.expr:
(
  count by (GPU_I_PROFILE) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!=""})
  - (count by (GPU_I_PROFILE) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!="", exported_pod!=""}) or vector(0))
)

panels.title: Free MIG Slices by Profile

panels.description: Average GR engine active % across all slices of each MIG profile
panels.title: MIG Compute % by Profile

panels.description: Average VRAM utilization % across all slices of each MIG profile
panels.title: MIG VRAM % by Profile

panels.description: Free slice count per profile over time. Sustained zero free slices for a profile = that profile is a scheduling bottleneck.

targets.expr:
(
  count by (GPU_I_PROFILE) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!=""})
  - (count by (GPU_I_PROFILE) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{GPU_I_ID!="", exported_pod!=""}) or vector(0))
)

panels.title: Free MIG Slices Over Time

panels.description: Sum of GR engine active % across all devices per namespace. This is GPU compute time consumed, not VRAM — the billing-relevant signal.
panels.title: GPU Compute Share by Namespace (Current)

panels.description: Average compute share per namespace over the selected time range. Use 7d or 30d for capacity planning and chargeback decisions.
panels.title: GPU Compute Share by Namespace (Range Avg)

panels.description: VRAM consumed per namespace. Compare with compute share: a namespace with high VRAM but low compute share has idle models loaded.

targets.expr: sum by (exported_namespace) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED{exported_pod!=""})

panels.title: VRAM Share by Namespace

panels.description: Compute share per namespace over time. Use with 7-30d range. Growing lines = teams ramping up; flat lines with high VRAM = idle allocations.
panels.title: GPU Compute Share by Namespace Over Time

panels.description: GPUs with VRAM in use but compute < 5%. These are the primary waste candidates.

targets.expr:
count(
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) > 0)
  unless
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100) > 5)
) or vector(0)

panels.title: Idle-Allocated GPUs Now

panels.description: Number of GPUs holding VRAM but below 5% compute activity. Sustained non-zero values indicate workloads should be scaled down or idle pods reclaimed.

targets.expr:
count(
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) > 0)
  unless
  (avg by (gpu, GPU_I_ID, UUID) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100) > 5)
) or vector(0)

panels.title: Idle-Allocated GPU Count Over Time

panels.description: All GPU-holding workloads sorted by Compute % ASC. Rows with low Compute % and high VRAM are reclaim candidates. Threshold: Compute < 10% = critical waste.
panels.title: Workload Efficiency — Reclaim Candidates

panels.description: Average cluster compute utilization over time. Use 7d-30d range. Consistent growth toward 70%+ signals need for more capacity.

targets.expr: avg(DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)

panels.title: Cluster Compute % Trend

panels.description: Average cluster VRAM utilization over time. VRAM trending toward 85%+ while compute stays low = over-provisioned models or idle inference servers.
panels.title: Cluster VRAM % Trend

panels.description: Absolute VRAM consumption in GB over time. Growing steadily = organic demand growth. Sharp drops = workloads evicted or completed.

targets.expr: sum(DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED) / 1024

panels.title: Total VRAM In Use (GB) Trend

panels.description: % of GPUs with GPU_UTIL < 5% over time. Increasing idle % with constant device count = efficiency degradation.

targets.expr:
(
  count(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL < 5)
  / count(DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL)
) * 100 or vector(0)

panels.title: Idle GPU % Trend

panels.description: Median cluster compute % over selected time range

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.50, avg(DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)[$__range:])

panels.title: Compute P50 (Range)

panels.description: 90th percentile cluster compute % over selected time range

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.90, avg(DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)[$__range:])

panels.title: Compute P90 (Range)

panels.description: 95th percentile cluster compute % — your peak demand signal

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, avg(DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)[$__range:])

panels.title: Compute P95 (Range)

panels.description: Workloads consuming the most VRAM. Large footprints limit co-location and MIG scheduling options.
panels.title: Top 10 VRAM-Consuming Workloads

panels.description: Workloads consuming the most GPU compute. These are the heaviest compute users — optimization here has the highest impact.
panels.title: Top 10 Compute-Consuming Workloads

panels.description: Max node compute % minus min node compute %. >20% = scheduling skew worth investigating.

targets.expr:
max(avg by (Hostname) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100))
- min(avg by (Hostname) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100))

panels.title: Compute Imbalance

panels.description: Average GR engine active % per node. Large gaps between nodes indicate scheduling imbalance.

targets.expr: avg by (Hostname) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)

panels.title: Compute % per Node

panels.description: Average VRAM utilization % per node. Cross with compute: node with high VRAM + low compute = idle models sitting on that node.
panels.title: VRAM % per Node

panels.description: Per-node compute % over time. Nodes that consistently run hotter than others may have affinity rules concentrating workloads, or devices that are not exporting metrics correctly.

targets.expr: avg by (Hostname) (DCGM_FI_PROF_GR_ENGINE_ACTIVE * 100)

panels.title: Compute % per Node Over Time

title: GPU Utilization Intelligence
